---
title: "Security, Trust, and Warding Words"
---

# Security, Trust, and Warding Words

Range: `0969-1063`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 19    |
| stub     | 76    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                       | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                        |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------ | ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0969  | `STUB` [ABAC](#rune-0969)                  | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0970  | `STUB` [access control](#rune-0970)        | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0971  | `STUB` [account](#rune-0971)               | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 0972  | `STUB` [ACL](#rune-0972)                   | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 0973  | `STUB` [allowlist](#rune-0973)             | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0974  | [attestation](#rune-0974)                  | authored   | A proof that a thing is what it claims to be or was produced the way it claims. It matters most when trust cannot be assumed.                  |
| 0975  | `STUB` [audit](#rune-0975)                 | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0976  | [authenticate](#rune-0976)                 | authored   | Prove who is making the claim before deciding what the claimant may do. Shadow: trusting names that have not earned entrance.                  |
| 0977  | `STUB` [authentication](#rune-0977)        | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 0978  | [authorization](#rune-0978)                | authored   | After identity is known, what acts are permitted? Shadow: accidental omnipotence hidden behind a successful login.                             |
| 0979  | [availability](#rune-0979)                 | authored   | A promise word that says the system can still be reached when called upon.                                                                     |
| 0980  | `STUB` [bearer token](#rune-0980)          | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 0981  | `STUB` [blast radius](#rune-0981)          | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 0982  | `STUB` [blocklist](#rune-0982)             | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0983  | `STUB` [boundary defense](#rune-0983)      | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 0984  | `STUB` [capability {Sec}](#rune-0984)      | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 0985  | `STUB` [certificate {Sec}](#rune-0985)     | stub       | A delegated-trust word; it lets one party assert another's identity by signed authority.                                                       |
| 0986  | `STUB` [challenge](#rune-0986)             | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0987  | `STUB` [cipher](#rune-0987)                | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0988  | `STUB` [claim](#rune-0988)                 | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 0989  | `STUB` [client secret](#rune-0989)         | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 0990  | [code signing](#rune-0990)                 | authored   | Cryptographically bind an artifact to a trusted producer.                                                                                      |
| 0991  | `STUB` [compliance](#rune-0991)            | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0992  | `STUB` [confidentiality](#rune-0992)       | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 0993  | `STUB` [credential {Sec}](#rune-0993)      | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0994  | `STUB` [cross-site scripting](#rune-0994)  | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 0995  | `STUB` [cryptographic nonce](#rune-0995)   | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 0996  | `STUB` [CSRF](#rune-0996)                  | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 0997  | `STUB` [decrypt {Sec}](#rune-0997)         | stub       | An unsealing word; protected meaning becomes legible again under the right key.                                                                |
| 0998  | [defense in depth](#rune-0998)             | authored   | Stack multiple protective layers so one failure is not total failure.                                                                          |
| 0999  | `STUB` [deny-by-default](#rune-0999)       | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1000  | `STUB` [digest {Sec}](#rune-1000)          | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1001  | `STUB` [enclave](#rune-1001)               | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1002  | [encrypt {Sec}](#rune-1002)                | authored   | Make plaintext unreadable without the right key.                                                                                               |
| 1003  | `STUB` [entropy](#rune-1003)               | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1004  | `STUB` [exfiltration](#rune-1004)          | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1005  | `STUB` [exploit](#rune-1005)               | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1006  | `STUB` [harden](#rune-1006)                | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1007  | [hash {Sec}](#rune-1007)                   | authored   | Arbitrary input reduced to a fixed fingerprint. Use it for identity, integrity, bucketing, and change detection; never mistake it for secrecy. |
| 1008  | `STUB` [HMAC](#rune-1008)                  | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1009  | `STUB` [identity](#rune-1009)              | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1010  | `STUB` [incident](#rune-1010)              | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1011  | `STUB` [injection](#rune-1011)             | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 1012  | [integrity](#rune-1012)                    | authored   | Confidence that data has not been silently altered.                                                                                            |
| 1013  | `STUB` [isolate {Sec}](#rune-1013)         | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1014  | [key {Sec}](#rune-1014)                    | authored   | The secret or public material that makes cryptographic operations possible.                                                                    |
| 1015  | `STUB` [key exchange](#rune-1015)          | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1016  | [key rotation](#rune-1016)                 | authored   | Replace active keys before age or compromise turns them rotten.                                                                                |
| 1017  | [least privilege](#rune-1017)              | authored   | Give only the authority required for the present act, no more. It is one of the simplest words for reducing future regret.                     |
| 1018  | `STUB` [login](#rune-1018)                 | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1019  | `STUB` [mTLS {Sec}](#rune-1019)            | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1020  | `STUB` [mutual authentication](#rune-1020) | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1021  | `STUB` [nonce](#rune-1021)                 | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 1022  | `STUB` [passkey](#rune-1022)               | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1023  | `STUB` [password](#rune-1023)              | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 1024  | `STUB` [patch {Sec}](#rune-1024)           | stub       | A repair word; a bounded alteration is applied to an existing body.                                                                            |
| 1025  | `STUB` [pepper](#rune-1025)                | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1026  | `STUB` [permission {Sec}](#rune-1026)      | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1027  | `STUB` [phishing](#rune-1027)              | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1028  | [policy](#rune-1028)                       | authored   | The declared rule by which a class of cases is decided. Good policy reduces arbitrary judgment; bad policy merely freezes confusion.           |
| 1029  | `STUB` [principal](#rune-1029)             | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1030  | `STUB` [private key](#rune-1030)           | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1031  | `STUB` [privilege escalation](#rune-1031)  | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1032  | `STUB` [proof of possession](#rune-1032)   | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1033  | `STUB` [public key](#rune-1033)            | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1034  | `STUB` [quarantine](#rune-1034)            | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1035  | `STUB` [rate limiting](#rune-1035)         | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1036  | `STUB` [replay attack](#rune-1036)         | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1037  | [revocation](#rune-1037)                   | authored   | The act of withdrawing trust from credentials or keys.                                                                                         |
| 1038  | `STUB` [role {Sec}](#rune-1038)            | stub       | An invocation word; it frames what kind of actor the model should pretend to be.                                                               |
| 1039  | `STUB` [root of trust](#rune-1039)         | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 1040  | `STUB` [rotate](#rune-1040)                | stub       | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                        |
| 1041  | `STUB` [salt](#rune-1041)                  | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1042  | [sandbox {Sec}](#rune-1042)                | authored   | Constrain code inside a smaller, safer prison.                                                                                                 |
| 1043  | `STUB` [scope {Sec}](#rune-1043)           | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1044  | [secret {Sec}](#rune-1044)                 | authored   | Information whose power comes from staying hidden.                                                                                             |
| 1045  | `STUB` [secure enclave](#rune-1045)        | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1046  | `STUB` [security boundary](#rune-1046)     | stub       | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                         |
| 1047  | `STUB` [session fixation](#rune-1047)      | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1048  | `STUB` [session token](#rune-1048)         | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1049  | `STUB` [sign {Sec}](#rune-1049)            | stub       | A sealing word; origin or integrity is bound cryptographically to content.                                                                     |
| 1050  | [signature {Sec}](#rune-1050)              | authored   | Identity or intent made checkable through cryptographic proof. Useful wherever trust must survive distance and replay.                         |
| 1051  | `STUB` [single sign-on](#rune-1051)        | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1052  | `STUB` [spoofing](#rune-1052)              | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1053  | `STUB` [SSRF](#rune-1053)                  | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1054  | [threat model](#rune-1054)                 | authored   | A structured view of likely attackers, assets, and failure paths.                                                                              |
| 1055  | `STUB` [token {Sec}](#rune-1055)           | stub       | A countable unit of text or symbol used for parsing, modeling, or encoding.                                                                    |
| 1056  | `STUB` [trust anchor](#rune-1056)          | stub       | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                      |
| 1057  | `STUB` [trust boundary](#rune-1057)        | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1058  | `STUB` [validate {Sec}](#rune-1058)        | stub       | A gatekeeping word; inputs or outputs are checked against an expected form.                                                                    |
| 1059  | `STUB` [verification {Sec}](#rune-1059)    | stub       | A truth-checking word that asks not merely for output, but for justified output.                                                               |
| 1060  | `STUB` [vulnerability](#rune-1060)         | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1061  | `STUB` [ward](#rune-1061)                  | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1062  | `STUB` [webhook signing](#rune-1062)       | stub       | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                |
| 1063  | [zero trust](#rune-1063)                   | authored   | Assume no network location or ambient context deserves automatic trust.                                                                        |

## Entries

### [0969] ABAC {#rune-0969}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0970] access control {#rune-0970}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0971] account {#rune-0971}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0972] ACL {#rune-0972}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0973] allowlist {#rune-0973}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0974] attestation {#rune-0974}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A proof that a thing is what it claims to be or was produced the way it claims. It matters most when trust cannot be assumed.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Evidence that a thing is what it claims to be or was produced the way it claims.

### [0975] audit {#rune-0975}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0976] authenticate {#rune-0976}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Prove who is making the claim before deciding what the claimant may do.

**Shadow:** trusting names that have not earned entrance.

**Pocket gloss:** Prove who is making the claim.

### [0977] authentication {#rune-0977}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0978] authorization {#rune-0978}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** After identity is known, what acts are permitted?

**Shadow:** accidental omnipotence hidden behind a successful login.

**Pocket gloss:** Decide what an already identified actor may do.

### [0979] availability {#rune-0979}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A promise word that says the system can still be reached when called upon.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0980] bearer token {#rune-0980}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0981] blast radius {#rune-0981}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0982] blocklist {#rune-0982}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0983] boundary defense {#rune-0983}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0984] capability {Sec} {#rune-0984}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0985] certificate {Sec} {#rune-0985}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A delegated-trust word; it lets one party assert another's identity by signed authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0986] challenge {#rune-0986}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0987] cipher {#rune-0987}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0988] claim {#rune-0988}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0989] client secret {#rune-0989}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0990] code signing {#rune-0990}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Cryptographically bind an artifact to a trusted producer.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Cryptographically bind an artifact to a trusted producer.

### [0991] compliance {#rune-0991}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0992] confidentiality {#rune-0992}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0993] credential {Sec} {#rune-0993}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0994] cross-site scripting {#rune-0994}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0995] cryptographic nonce {#rune-0995}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0996] CSRF {#rune-0996}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0997] decrypt {Sec} {#rune-0997}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An unsealing word; protected meaning becomes legible again under the right key.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [0998] defense in depth {#rune-0998}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Stack multiple protective layers so one failure is not total failure.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Stack multiple protective layers so one failure is not total failure.

### [0999] deny-by-default {#rune-0999}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1000] digest {Sec} {#rune-1000}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1001] enclave {#rune-1001}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1002] encrypt {Sec} {#rune-1002}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Make plaintext unreadable without the right key.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Make plaintext unreadable without the right key.

### [1003] entropy {#rune-1003}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1004] exfiltration {#rune-1004}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1005] exploit {#rune-1005}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1006] harden {#rune-1006}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1007] hash {Sec} {#rune-1007}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Arbitrary input reduced to a fixed fingerprint. Use it for identity, integrity, bucketing, and change detection; never mistake it for secrecy.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Reduce arbitrary input to a fixed fingerprint.

### [1008] HMAC {#rune-1008}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1009] identity {#rune-1009}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1010] incident {#rune-1010}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1011] injection {#rune-1011}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1012] integrity {#rune-1012}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Confidence that data has not been silently altered.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Confidence that data has not been silently altered.

### [1013] isolate {Sec} {#rune-1013}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1014] key {Sec} {#rune-1014}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The secret or public material that makes cryptographic operations possible.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** The secret or public material that makes cryptographic operations possible.

### [1015] key exchange {#rune-1015}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1016] key rotation {#rune-1016}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Replace active keys before age or compromise turns them rotten.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Replace active keys before age or compromise turns them rotten.

### [1017] least privilege {#rune-1017}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Give only the authority required for the present act, no more. It is one of the simplest words for reducing future regret.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Grant only the authority required for the present act.

### [1018] login {#rune-1018}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1019] mTLS {Sec} {#rune-1019}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1020] mutual authentication {#rune-1020}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1021] nonce {#rune-1021}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1022] passkey {#rune-1022}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1023] password {#rune-1023}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1024] patch {Sec} {#rune-1024}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A repair word; a bounded alteration is applied to an existing body.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1025] pepper {#rune-1025}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1026] permission {Sec} {#rune-1026}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1027] phishing {#rune-1027}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1028] policy {#rune-1028}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The declared rule by which a class of cases is decided. Good policy reduces arbitrary judgment; bad policy merely freezes confusion.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** A declared rule for how a class of cases is decided.

### [1029] principal {#rune-1029}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1030] private key {#rune-1030}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1031] privilege escalation {#rune-1031}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1032] proof of possession {#rune-1032}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1033] public key {#rune-1033}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1034] quarantine {#rune-1034}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1035] rate limiting {#rune-1035}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1036] replay attack {#rune-1036}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1037] revocation {#rune-1037}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The act of withdrawing trust from credentials or keys.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** The act of withdrawing trust from credentials or keys.

### [1038] role {Sec} {#rune-1038}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An invocation word; it frames what kind of actor the model should pretend to be.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1039] root of trust {#rune-1039}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1040] rotate {#rune-1040}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1041] salt {#rune-1041}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1042] sandbox {Sec} {#rune-1042}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Constrain code inside a smaller, safer prison.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Constrain code inside a smaller, safer prison.

### [1043] scope {Sec} {#rune-1043}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1044] secret {Sec} {#rune-1044}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Information whose power comes from staying hidden.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Information whose power comes from staying hidden.

### [1045] secure enclave {#rune-1045}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1046] security boundary {#rune-1046}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1047] session fixation {#rune-1047}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1048] session token {#rune-1048}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1049] sign {Sec} {#rune-1049}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A sealing word; origin or integrity is bound cryptographically to content.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1050] signature {Sec} {#rune-1050}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Identity or intent made checkable through cryptographic proof. Useful wherever trust must survive distance and replay.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Identity or intent made checkable through cryptographic proof.

### [1051] single sign-on {#rune-1051}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1052] spoofing {#rune-1052}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1053] SSRF {#rune-1053}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1054] threat model {#rune-1054}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A structured view of likely attackers, assets, and failure paths.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** A structured view of likely attackers, assets, and failure paths.

### [1055] token {Sec} {#rune-1055}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A countable unit of text or symbol used for parsing, modeling, or encoding.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1056] trust anchor {#rune-1056}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1057] trust boundary {#rune-1057}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1058] validate {Sec} {#rune-1058}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A gatekeeping word; inputs or outputs are checked against an expected form.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1059] verification {Sec} {#rune-1059}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-checking word that asks not merely for output, but for justified output.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1060] vulnerability {#rune-1060}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1061] ward {#rune-1061}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1062] webhook signing {#rune-1062}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

### [1063] zero trust {#rune-1063}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Assume no network location or ambient context deserves automatic trust.

**Shadow:** misplaced trust can grant authority, expose secrets, or make identity look stronger than it is.

**Pocket gloss:** Assume no network location or ambient context deserves automatic trust.
